# Transcrição: 10-Promote Impact to Team

- **Fonte:** 10-Promote Impact to Team
- **Duração:** 03:18
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:05]

You want to promote the impact of your new ownership of your new business. That means getting to know each of your employees and painting a clear picture of the impact you'll have on their lives. If they'll help you accomplish your vision for the business, then you can help them accomplish their goals through the business. See, you have to understand their goals though. And then you have to plot a course to achieve them through the vision of your business. When you align their goals with your goals, when you win, they win when they win you win. Make it clear to your team that the goals of your business are dependent on them. That's why you need to align their goals with the business goals and the business's goals with their goals. Make it clear that the path to building a successful business includes their contribution and the rewards that come from it. Explain to your new team that your number one initiative is to bring the resources, invest in the things that give them the skills to meet the business's new standards and objectives. This way they know that you're willing to invest in them so they can invest in the business. To do that, let them know that you'll be focusing your time working with them on clarifying the vision, helping them get better and better at asking great questions and to be extremely disciplined at coming at problems with solution mindset versus a problem mindset. Specifically, that you want them to come to you with anything they see as an opportunity or a problem in the business and just ask them to have some suggestions or solutions on how to fix it. It is the intelligence inside your business that can tell you how to do things bigger, better and faster to create more value. Create a mindset in your organization that you value that more than anything else. Then you have to make yourself available and actually lead them in the way that you're promising. And again, they need to know that by participating with you and bringing this to you, they get to win when you win. The business hits its goals and objectives, they hit their goals and objectives. And it should be that if they're hitting their goals and objectives, the business must be hitting its goals and objectives. The more you can align your employees to the business objectives, the less they'll be out looking for other jobs. What you want them to do is understand what they need to contribute as far as their skill sets to complement the business and create the impact and the value that you want for the business. Because all of this for the business. You see all this translates over to your clients that work with you and how their lives are impacted positively by the business's success and profitability. If your employees are aligned with the business, if you're aligned with your employees and you and the business and your employees are aligned with the deliverable, then your clients

## [03:05 - 03:18]

will be aligned with doing more business with you. This is how you entirely align a business. Your goals, the business's goals, your employees' goals and the goals of the people you serve winning together. That's what we call a 10X community.
